content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (Apr 4, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on April 4, 2026, focused on expanding the framework's security and trust ecosystem through new plugin proposals. Community discussions centered on integrating cryptographic identity, behavioral fingerprinting, and tool-call authorization layers to enhance agent safety.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS
*No code changes, refactors, or feature additions were merged into the repository on this date.*

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*None.*

## CLOSED ISSUES
*None.*

## NEW ISSUES

### Security and Plugin Proposals
*   [#6707](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6707): Proposal for `@sint/eliza-plugin`, which introduces a formal authorization layer for agent tool calls using capability tokens to enforce security policies.
*   [#6706](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6706): Proposal for `@elizaos/plugin-safeagent`, designed to perform pre-trade safety checks (such as honeypot and rug pull detection) for agents operating in crypto markets.

## ACTIVE ISSUES

### Agent Identity and Trust Frameworks
*   [#6688](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6688): Discussion regarding the integration of `AgentID` for cryptographic identity and trust levels. 
    *   **Key Points:** Contributors are debating the merits of static trust levels (L0-L4) versus dynamic behavioral fingerprinting. 
    *   **Challenges:** Participants are comparing the `AgentID` approach with the `SINT Protocol`'s "Avatar Layer," which uses a Composite Safety-Model Latency (CSML) metric to auto-adjust agent trust tiers based on real-time behavioral evidence.
    *   **Progress:** There is an ongoing effort to align evidence schemas between different security protocols to ensure agents can maintain consistent behavioral history across various systems.
